income_thresholds.description: BPL and NFSA families with annual income below Rs 5 lakh are eligible for free coverage. Rs 2 lakh for general hospitalization plus Rs 2 lakh for critical illness (total Rs 4 lakh per family per year).

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Meghalaya are eligible for MHIS
Must be a BPL or NFSA cardholder to qualify for MHIS. Families not listed under BPL/NFSA are not eligible.
Families with annual income above Rs 5 lakh are not eligible
Families already covered under CGHS or ESIC are excluded
General hospitalization capped at Rs 2 lakh. Critical illness has separate Rs 2 lakh limit. Total combined cap is Rs 4 lakh per family per year.

family_criteria.family_definition: Family of up to 5 members as listed on BPL/NFSA/ration card. Family includes head of family, spouse, and up to 3 dependent children. Additional members may be covered on case-by-case basis.

covered_procedures: Critical illness treatment (cancer, heart disease, kidney failure, stroke)
Diagnostics and lab tests during hospitalization
excluded_procedures: Dental treatment (unless related to covered surgery)

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Required for identity verification and e-KYC

required_documents.name: BPL Card / NFSA Card
required_documents.notes: Mandatory proof of BPL/NFSA status. Must be valid and issued by Meghalaya state government.

required_documents.name: ST/SC Certificate
required_documents.local_name_hi: अनुसूचित जनजाति / अनुसूचित जाति प्रमाण पत्र
required_documents.notes: If applicable. Meghalaya has a large tribal population. ST certificate helps with faster processing.

required_documents.name: Residency Proof
required_documents.notes: Must prove current residence in Meghalaya

enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit the nearest empanelled hospital, district hospital, or MHIS enrollment camp with required documents.
Step 2: Submit Aadhaar card, BPL/NFSA card, residency proof, and photograph.
Step 3: Undergo biometric verification (fingerprint/iris scan) for e-KYC.
Step 4: MHIS smart card is generated with enrolled family member details.
Step 5: Card is activated. Present at any empanelled hospital in Meghalaya for cashless treatment.
Step 6: General hospitalization covered up to Rs 2 lakh. Critical illness covered up to additional Rs 2 lakh (total Rs 4 lakh per year).
description_for_embedding: Megha Health Insurance Scheme (MHIS) is Meghalaya's state health insurance scheme providing Rs 4 lakh per family per year, split into Rs 2 lakh for general hospitalization and Rs 2 lakh for critical illness. The scheme targets BPL and NFSA families in Meghalaya with annual income below Rs 5 lakh. MHIS covers secondary and tertiary care including hospitalization, surgeries, maternity care, neonatal care, and critical illness treatment at empanelled hospitals. Enrollment requires BPL/NFSA card, Aadhaar, and residency proof. The scheme particularly benefits tribal communities, farmers, plantation workers, and mining workers in Meghalaya. Smart card based cashless treatment across empanelled hospitals.
